skill.name: web-search-scraper-api-skill
skill.description: Extracts complete Markdown content from any website URL via the BrowserAct Web Search Scraper API. The agent applies this skill when users need to scrape, parse, or download web page content as clean Markdown text.
skill.summary: Converts any public web page URL into clean Markdown content using the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 37 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives in context. The Python script is a standard API client that makes HTTP requests to a single legitimate endpoint (api.browseract.com). The env_access findings correspond to reading a BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables, which is a standard and necessary pattern for authenticating with the API service. The external_commands findings are markdown code block examples in SKILL.md, not executable code. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance findings had no supporting evidence in the actual file contents. The skill performs its documented purpose with no malicious intent or data exfiltration patterns detected.

low_findings.title: External Commands in Markdown Code Blocks
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash command examples inside markdown code fences. These are documentation examples, not executed commands. The static analyzer misinterpreted markdown backtick syntax as shell backtick execution. No actual command injection risk exists.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables. This is a standard and secure pattern for API credential management. The API key is used only to authenticate with the documented BrowserAct API service at api.browseract.com.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to Legitimate API Endpoint
low_findings.description: All HTTP requests target api.browseract.com, a documented third-party web scraping API. The requests are for starting tasks, polling status, and retrieving results. No data exfiltration or communication with unknown endpoints.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Detection)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md lines 3, 10, 29, and 47 for weak cryptographic algorithms. These lines contain markdown headings, descriptions, and parameter documentation. No cryptographic operations exist in this skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Detection)
low_findings.description: SKILL.md lines 55-56 describe error handling logic for checking if API responses contain 'Invalid authorization'. This is standard error message parsing, not system reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Extract Web Page Content as Markdown
content.value_statement: Copying content from websites is slow and error-prone. This skill automatically extracts clean Markdown text from any public URL using the BrowserAct API, giving you structured content without HTML noise.
actual_capabilities: Converts any public website URL into clean Markdown content
Handles task polling with automatic retry and timeout logic
Detects and reports invalid or expired API keys with clear error messages
Returns structured text without HTML tags, CSS artifacts, or JavaScript
Supports all HTTP and HTTPS URLs including articles, blogs, and documentation pages
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key configured as an environment variable
Cannot access pages behind login forms or authentication gates
Task execution may take several minutes for complex or large pages
Extraction quality depends on the target page structure and content format

use_cases.title: Research Article Collection
use_cases.description: A journalist researching a topic can extract the full text of multiple news articles into Markdown files for offline analysis and citation.

use_cases.title: Documentation Backup
use_cases.description: A developer can scrape technical documentation pages and save them as Markdown for local reference, offline reading, or integration into internal wikis.

use_cases.title: Content Migration
use_cases.description: A content manager migrating a website can extract existing blog posts as Markdown to import into a new CMS without manual copy-paste work.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Single Article
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the content from this URL as Markdown: https://example.com/article
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a single URL and wants the full page content

prompt_templates.title: Extract and Save Multiple Pages
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to extract content from these three URLs and save each as a separate Markdown file: url1, url2, url3
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to batch extract content from multiple URLs

prompt_templates.title: Extract Documentation for Offline Use
prompt_templates.prompt: Please scrape the documentation at https://docs.example.com/guide and save it as a Markdown file so I can read it offline.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to save online documentation for offline reference

prompt_templates.title: Monitor Page Content Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the current content of https://example.com/pricing and compare it to the version from last week to identify what changed.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to track changes to a specific page over time

output_examples.output: Returns the full Wikipedia article on Markdown converted to clean Markdown format, including headings, paragraphs, lists, and code blocks stripped of HTML formatting.

output_examples.input: Scrape the blog post at https://example.com/blog/hello-world
output_examples.output: Returns the blog post body content in Markdown with preserved heading hierarchy, links converted to Markdown format, and images referenced by their alt text and URLs.

best_practices: Always provide the full URL including the protocol (https://) to ensure the API can access the target page correctly.
Verify that the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before requesting extraction to avoid authentication errors.
Monitor the terminal output during extraction for status updates; the task may take several minutes for large pages.
anti_patterns: Do not use relative URLs or URLs without a protocol scheme, as the API requires complete absolute URLs to function.
Do not retry extraction immediately after a failure; check the error message first since an invalid API key requires manual user intervention.
Do not attempt to extract pages behind login forms or paywalls, as the skill cannot authenticate or bypass access controls.

faq.question: What API key is needed for this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Get one from the BrowserAct Console at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations and set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: How long does a typical content extraction take?
faq.answer: Extraction time varies based on page complexity. Simple pages complete in under a minute, while complex pages with many resources may take several minutes.

faq.question: What types of URLs are supported?
faq.answer: Any public HTTP or HTTPS URL is supported. This includes news articles, blog posts, documentation pages, and general website content.

faq.question: Can this skill access pages behind a login or paywall?
faq.answer: No. The skill can only access publicly available web pages. It cannot authenticate, bypass paywalls, or access password-protected content.

faq.question: Is the extracted Markdown output clean and usable?
faq.answer: Yes. The output contains the main page content structured as Markdown without HTML tags, CSS styles, JavaScript, or navigation elements.

faq.question: Can I extract content from multiple URLs at once?
faq.answer: Each extraction handles one URL at a time. For multiple URLs, run the skill separately for each URL and save each result to a different file.
